---
title: "Coral Bleaching Risk Notebook"
description: "An exploratory data analysis notebook examining environmental drivers of coral bleaching risk using sea surface temperature and DHW data."
date: "2025-04-01"
categories: [marine science, data analysis, jupyter, python]
image: ../images/bleaching-risk.jpg
---

## Overview

Coral bleaching occurs when water temperatures exceed a threshold for an extended 
period, measured in Degree Heating Weeks (DHW). This notebook explores publicly 
available sea surface temperature (SST) and DHW data to identify patterns and 
risk factors associated with bleaching events.

## Data Sources

- NOAA Coral Reef Watch SST and DHW products
- ReefBase bleaching observation records
- AIMS Long Term Monitoring Program data (planned)

## Analysis

### Exploratory Data Analysis
- SST anomaly trends over time
- DHW accumulation patterns by region
- Correlation with reported bleaching events

### Visualization
- Time series plots of thermal stress
- Spatial maps of bleaching risk
- Interactive plots with Plotly

## Tools Used

- Python (pandas, xarray, matplotlib, plotly)
- Jupyter Notebooks
- NOAA Coral Reef Watch API

## Status

🚧 *In progress — data acquisition and EDA underway.*

## Notebook

*Jupyter notebook will be embedded here once complete.*
